name: Jin Qian
tagline: Researching environmental governance, more-than-human politics, and China's wildlife and food systems.
bio: Jin Qian is a researcher working at the intersection of environmental governance, more-than-human relations, and China's wildlife and food systems. Her recent work examines wild animal farming, ecological civilization, and the politics of nature-based solutions across the Global South. She holds a PhD candidacy at Wageningen University & Research.
longBio:
Jin Qian's research traces how humans and other animals are governed together — and apart — through state policy, market design, and everyday practice. Drawing on ethnographic fieldwork in China and comparative analysis across the Global South, her work asks how regulatory categories ("wildlife," "livestock," "endangered") shape both ecological outcomes and the moral status of the creatures they describe.

Her published work examines China's wild animal farming sector and the more-than-human practices that sustain it, post-COVID shifts in wildlife policy, the construction of China's climate-forestry nexus under the banner of ecological civilization, and the export of nature-based solutions to Asia, Africa, and Latin America. She has published in *One Earth*, *Global Environmental Change*, and the *Journal of Rural Studies*.

She is currently a PhD candidate at Wageningen University & Research.

podcast.name: 有点豆腐 Slightly Tofu
podcast.description: Chinese-language podcast on animal rights, veganism, and abolitionism
